own_review_points: hasCorrelatedSubquery in AbstractSqlAstTranslator may be incorrect: correlations can live in an UPDATE's SET clause, not just predicate/from - missing branch (human ref)
NestedCorrelationChecker null check on previousAliases needed (human ref) - potential NPE
Heavy duplication between MariaDB core and legacy translators (and between the three checker classes) - extract shared logic
currentLevelAliases != null used as implicit 'in subquery' guard - non-obvious / fragile
Statement now extends SqlAstNode - verify no instanceof-exclusion code breaks
Version gate: single-table fallback only needed before MariaDB 11.1 (self-join rewrite handles it after) - human ref
notes: v8's dead-code finding on hasCorrelatedSubquery and the duplication observation are valid and substantive (two good). The other three points are hedged 'verify/consider documenting' restatements. Crucially it missed the two real correctness concerns the maintainer raised: the SET-clause correlation gap in hasCorrelatedSubquery (it noted the method is unused but not that the logic is wrong) and the missing null check in NestedCorrelationChecker, plus the 11.1 version-gating refinement. Net slightly negative.
